Header file for loading magic strings in the JerryScript mbed launcher.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines an interface for setting up the JerryScript engine in an mbed environment. It includes a guard to prevent multiple inclusions and imports the JerryScript API through the `jerry-core/jerry-api.h` header. The file declares a single function, [`jsmbed_js_load_magic_strings`](<#jsmbed_js_load_magic_strings>), which is intended to load predefined strings, known as "magic strings," into the JerryScript engine. This setup is necessary for initializing and configuring the JerryScript environment to handle specific string operations efficiently.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `jerry-core/jerry-api.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### jsmbed\_js\_load\_magic\_strings<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jsmbed_js_load_magic_strings}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/mbedos5/jerryscript-mbed/jerryscript-mbed-launcher/setup.h#L20>)

Registers and sets magic strings in the global object.
- **Description**: Use this function to register and set predefined magic strings in the global object of the JerryScript engine. It must be called when `jsmbed_js_magic_string_count` is greater than zero. This function initializes the magic strings and their corresponding values, making them available in the global scope. It is important to ensure that the JerryScript engine is properly initialized before calling this function. The function does not perform any action if there are no magic strings to register.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jsmbed_js_load_magic_strings`](<source/setup.cpp.md#jsmbed_js_load_magic_strings>)  (Implementation)
